assumptions: Please act as a professional verilog designer.

Implement a module for pulse detection. The 1-bit input signal data_in is a continuous input, which is triggered by clk. When "data_in" changes from 0 to 1 to 0(3 cycles), this is considered as a "pulse". The indicating signal dataout is 1 at the end cycle of the "pulse", and then returns to 0 until the corresponding pulse appears again. For example, if data_in is 01010(5 cycles), the data_out is 00101.
Declare the module pulse_detect with input and output ports as specified in the ports statement.
Declare a register state to keep track of the current state of the pulse detection process.
Inside an always block, sensitive to the positive edge of the clk signal and the negative edge of the rst_n signal, implement the logic for pulse detection and output generation.

In the reset condition (~rst_n), set the state register to the initial state and set the data_out output to 0, indicating no pulse. Continue the detection process for the remaining states. For each state, check the value of data_in and transition to the next state accordingly. If the current state satisfies the last state of a "pulse", set data_out to 1, indicating the end of a pulse. Otherwise, set data_out to 0.

checks.description: After reset release, observable outputs should reach a stable externally visible state before functional checking resumes.
notes: Reset functional oracle does not invent specific reset values.
checks.pass_condition: Observable outputs settle to a stable, externally usable state without relying on undocumented exact-cycle convergence.
checks.trigger_condition: When reset is released after a valid assertion interval.

checks.description: Observe externally visible state progress after a legal operation.
notes: Sequential functional oracle remains event-based unless a future contract explicitly provides fixed latency.
checks.pass_condition: At least one observable output or progress indicator eventually updates in a manner consistent with state progress; no exact-cycle update is required.
checks.trigger_condition: When the linked plan applies one or more legal sequential operations.

checks.description: Validate that a single pulse (0→1→0) generates a single-cycle data_out=1 at the third cycle.
checks.pass_condition: After a rising edge on data_in followed by a falling edge within two subsequent clock cycles, data_out becomes 1 in the cycle where data_in returns to 0, then returns to 0 thereafter.
checks.trigger_condition: When a legal pulse pattern is driven on data_in.

checks.description: Treat reset assertion/release as a control event and require reset-safe interface behavior.
notes: Reset is used as an anchor only; it is not treated as a business output.

notes: Reset oracle focuses on control-safe behavior instead of value-specific output claims.
